channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: After Just One Weekend, Michael Jackson Film Ranks Among 10 Highest-Grossing Music Biopics Worldwide: Find Out Where
item.description:
[Upbeat music fades out, and the host begins speaking]
Host: "Welcome to this week's episode of 'Box Office Buzz'. We're talking about the latest music biopic to hit the big screen - 'Michael', the film about the King of Pop, Michael Jackson. And what a debut it's had. According to the latest numbers, 'Michael' grossed a whopping $217.3 million worldwide in its opening weekend. That's already landed it at number 3 on Billboard's list of music biopics with the highest worldwide grosses.
[Short music transition]
Host: "But how high can it climb? Well, Michael Jackson has a history of topping the charts, dating back to his days with The Jackson 5. Their first number 1 hit, 'I Want You Back', was back in 1970. But to reach the top spot on this list, 'Michael' would need to surpass the all-time top-grossing music biopic, 'Bohemian Rhapsody', which raked in $910.8 million worldwide. That's a tall order, but we'll be keeping an eye on it.
[Short music transition]
Host: "In the US and Canada alone, 'Michael' grossed $97 million over the weekend, making it the highest opening-weekend domestic gross ever for a music biopic. That's more than four times what the 2009 concert film 'Michael Jackson's This Is It' made in its opening weekend. And interestingly, the co-producer of 'Michael', Graham King, also worked on 'Bohemian Rhapsody' and 'Jersey Boys'.
[Short music transition]
Host: "We'll be watching to see where 'Michael' lands on the list of highest-grossing music biopics. Will it make it to the top spot? Only time will tell. Thanks for tuning in to this week's episode of 'Box Office Buzz'. Join us next time for more updates from the world of entertainment."
[Upbeat music fades back in]
